title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. divaricatum
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. divaricatum, also known as the eastern poison ivy, is a subspecies of the Toxicodendron radicans plant, which is native to eastern North America. It is a woody, climbing vine with leaves that contain an oily sap called urushiol, which causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in many people. Despite its toxic properties, some indigenous cultures have traditionally used the plant for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, but caution is advised due to its highly allergenic nature.
